rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE LAWRENCE W. WONG 2011 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement
rulings.Rulings: This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Ross C. Moody. Petitioner Christopher J. Wong's Motion to Enforce Settlement Agreement is GRANTED. The Opposition to the instant motion focuses on difficulties Respondent Carole Wong had in refinancing 590 4th Avenue, and on perceived mistakes and misdeeds associated with the refinancing. However, the Settlement Agreement expressly and unambiguously stated that the obligation to pay the Settlement Amount was not tied to any financing. See Sparer Decl. Ex. A at 9 (3(a)) ["Respondent's obligation to pay the Settlement Amount set forth herein cannot be set aside or deferred, even if Respondent fails to secure funding of any loan or mortgage intended to fund this payment."]. Thus, under the express terms of the Settlement Agreement, even if Respondent Carole Wong had been unable to mortgage 590 4th Avenue for any reason, it would not have excused her contractual obligation to make the $1.5 million payment on time. Petitioner would have been entitled to obtain a judgment for the full Settlement Amount and accrued interest, with additional interest accruing. Id., Ex. A at 9 (3(b) and (c)). As a result, any claim of "mistake" based on the ownership of 590 4th Avenue does not excuse Respondent Carole Wong's performance or justify revision of the Agreement. On the record before it, the Court finds that Respondent Carole Wong has failed to show there was a mutual mistake of material fact, that it was impossible for her to perform under the contract, or that Petitioner Christopher J. Wong failed to cooperate under the Settlement Agreement. Respondent Carole Wong shall pay Petitioner Christopher J. Wong interest in the amount of $31,890.41 representing the interest that accrued before the Settlement Amount was paid on June 24, 2021, and $6.99 per day from June 25, 2021, until payment of the interest amounts set forth herein. Respondent Carole Wong shall further pay Petitioner Christopher J. Wong $26,212.97 in attorneys' fees and costs. All payments must be made within 15 days of entry of this Order. Petitioner Christopher J. Wong shall lodge with the clerk in Department 204 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order consistent with this tentative. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the court day before the hearing. Remote appearance is available in conformity with the current General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division, available on the court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/probate. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.
